Variant C  ·  HOMEPAGE — The Three Doors (new approach) The homepage IS the three-door choice — no hero stacked above it. Each door is its own bold heritage landing — Casa-Malka boldness applied to each space, not the site as a whole. Persistent "I · II · III" toggle in the header so users can horizontally jump between worlds on subpages. The "Built 1857 · Raided · Reborn" beats become a thin persistent footer ribbon, not a giant headline.

Thornton Distilling Co.
Reserve
American Single Malt Whisky · Est. 1857

Whisky of the American Prairie.

Drawn from the artesian well beneath our 1857 brewery. Malted from Illinois grain. Smoked with peat hand-harvested from the Mississippi. The first craft-malt single malt of the prairie.

Explore the Whisky Visit the Distillery →
Thornton · Illinois · Est. 1857

What you're looking at

Both variants are Direction C with a Prohibition twist, but they now do different jobs. Variant B was rewritten after I read your ASM brand brief — see notes below.

Variant A · Homepage hero (umbrella)

  • Daylight cream palette — confident, brand-storefront energy
  • Big headline visually wraps the bottle (Casa Malka, dialed back)
  • Leans into the venue's Prohibition-era drama (Dead Drop's tonal home)
  • Welcomes everyone — funnels into Spirits / Restaurant / Events
  • Note: The Capone hook may actually belong on the Dead Drop sub-page rather than the umbrella homepage. Worth deciding.

Variant B · ASM landing (Thornton Single Malt)

  • Cinematic dark palette — premium, contemplative, whisky-first
  • Headline rewritten per brief: "Whisky of the American Prairie."
  • Tone matches brief — Patient · Deliberate · Reverent
  • Leads with the unique terroir story (artesian well · Illinois malt · Mississippi peat)
  • Visual language: vintage Scotch labels + Farmer's Almanac + 1893 World's Fair

What the ASM brief changed

Your brief explicitly steers the ASM brand away from the Capone/Prohibition framing and toward Prairie / Artesian Well / 1857 heritage. Important shifts I'm now baking in:

  • Tone: Patient, Deliberate, Insightful — not bold/punchy. (Bold/punchy is Dead Drop's tonal lane.)
  • Hero assets: the historic building + the artesian well + local grain/peat. The brief explicitly calls these out as the brand's "Iconic Assets."
  • Product naming has changed: brief calls the flagship Four Malt (not "Signature Malt" as the current label PNG says), companion is Prairie Peat, single-cask LTO is Distiller's Select (not "Single Cask"). The bottle artwork in the folder may be outdated — please confirm.
  • Crest redesign noted: brief mentions revamping the crest to include the old Malt Tower in place of the cistern. Worth confirming whether this is done or in progress.
  • Counterpoint to Dead Drop: ASM and Dead Drop should look complimentary side-by-side but have distinct identities. Variant A (Capone, daylight, bold) and Variant B (Prairie, dark, reverent) demonstrate that contrast.

Proposed site architecture

  • Homepage — umbrella venue story (165+ years, three experiences). Variant A spirit works here, but maybe softer than "Seized by Capone."
  • Spirits experience — splits into Dead Drop (Prohibition-era boldness) and Thornton Single Malt (Prairie/heritage). Each has its own visual language.
  • Restaurant experience — its own page.
  • Events experience — its own page.

Open questions for you

  • Confirm product naming: is it now Four Malt / Prairie Peat / Distiller's Select? If yes, the existing bottle PNGs need new label artwork.
  • Has the crest been redesigned with the Malt Tower yet? If yes, drop the updated logo in the folder.
  • Is the homepage hook the Capone story or something softer and more heritage-forward? I have hunches both ways — happy to mock alternatives.
  • Any building / well / vault / still photography to drop into Thornton Distilling/Brand/photography/? That's the biggest single jump in mockup quality.

To react

Pick A and/or B (or "neither/hybrid"), tell me which open questions to nail down, and I'll iterate. Once we lock the hero direction, next sections to mock: Three Experiences strip, ASM landing page (full scroll), and Dead Drop landing page.
